params.name: Colossus of Rhodes
params.description: The Colossus is located at the entrance of the port of Rhodes in ancient Greece. Built in honor of the Sun God Helios, it was an engineering marvel of the ancient world. Standing at about thirty-three meters tall, the colossus was cast in bronze. It wore a radiant halo on its head and held a massive torch as a lighthouse that guided passing ships day and night until an earthquake brought it down. Despite its fall, the remnants of the colossus continue to awe future generations, becoming a signif …
